claim: Claude Code's CLI was previously called 'headless mode'; the -p (--print) flag runs it non-interactively, accepting a prompt and printing output to stdout, with --output-format stream-json for newline-delimited streaming events and --include-partial-messages for token deltas.
quote: The CLI was previously called "headless mode." The `-p` flag and all CLI options work the same way... Use `--output-format stream-json` with `--verbose` and `--include-partial-messages` to receive tokens as they're generated. Each line is a JSON object representing an event

claim: Sessions can be captured and resumed by ID for multi-turn workflows: `session_id=$(claude -p "Start a review" --output-format json | jq -r '.session_id')` then `claude -p "Continue that review" --resume "$session_id"`, which is the canonical pattern for a Slack bridge that maps Slack threads to Claude sessions.
quote:
session_id=$(claude -p "Start a review" --output-format json | jq -r '.session_id')
claude -p "Continue that review" --resume "$session_id"

claim: `--bare` mode skips auto-discovery of hooks, skills, plugins, MCP servers, and CLAUDE.md and is recommended for SDK/scripted calls; it requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (no OAuth/keychain) and will become the default for `-p`.
quote: Add `--bare` to reduce startup time by skipping auto-discovery of hooks, skills, plugins, MCP servers, auto memory, and CLAUDE.md... Bare mode skips OAuth and keychain reads. Anthropic authentication must come from `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`... `--bare` is the recommended mode for scripted and SDK calls

claim: The Claude Agent SDK is published as Python (`claude-agent-sdk` 0.1.48) and TypeScript (`@anthropic-ai/agent-sdk` 0.2.71) packages exposing the same agent loop, tool execution, and context management as Claude Code, suitable for being driven by an external event loop such as a Slack Events handler.
quote: The Claude Agent SDK is a Python and TypeScript library from Anthropic that provides the same agent loop, tools, and context management that power Claude Code, exposed as a programmable library

claim: The Python SDK uses sniffio-based dispatch to support both asyncio and Trio event loops, so it can be embedded inside a Slack Bolt async handler without runtime conflict.
quote: Trio compatibility recently restored. It uses sniffio-based dispatch to select the correct async primitive (asyncio.Task vs trio.lowlevel.spawn_system_task) at runtime

claim: Claude Code's `Notification` hook fires when Claude is waiting for input or permission and runs an arbitrary shell command — making it the natural wire for pushing 'awaiting approval' or 'task complete' status into a Slack channel via `curl` to a webhook URL.
quote: This hook uses the `Notification` event, which fires when Claude is waiting for input or permission... { "hooks": { "Notification": [ { "matcher": "", "hooks": [ { "type": "command", "command": "notify-send 'Claude Code' 'Claude Code needs your attention'" } ] } ] } }

claim: A `PermissionRequest` hook can answer a permission dialog programmatically by writing JSON to stdout (`{"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PermissionRequest", "decision": {"behavior": "allow"}}}`), which is the documented hook-based path for a Slack bridge to translate a button click into an approval.
quote: auto-approval requires your hook to write a JSON decision to stdout. A `PermissionRequest` hook fires when Claude Code is about to show a permission dialog, and returning `"behavior": "allow"` answers it on your behalf

claim: As of March 2026 Claude Code hooks support four handler types — command, http, prompt, and agent — and an `async: true` flag lets a hook fire to e.g. a Slack webhook in the background without blocking Claude's execution.
quote: As of March 2026, Claude Code Hooks supports 4 handler types: command, http, prompt, and agent... You can add `async: true` to run hooks in the background without blocking Claude's execution

claim: Slack ships an official MCP server at `https://mcp.slack.com/mcp` (HTTP transport, OAuth) that Claude Code installs via `/plugin install slack` or `claude plugin install slack`, exposing search/read/send/canvas tools — i.e., Slack is consumed as an MCP server *by* Claude Code, not the reverse.
quote: "type": "http", "url": "https://mcp.slack.com/mcp", "oauth": { "clientId": "1601185624273.8899143856786"... users run `/plugin install slack` within Claude Code or `claude plugin install slack`

claim: Anthropic's official 'Claude Code in Slack' product routes @Claude mentions in channels to a Claude Code session running on claude.ai/code (cloud), posts progress updates back to the Slack thread, and exposes 'View Session' / 'Create PR' / 'Retry as Code' / 'Change Repo' action buttons — but does not work in DMs and requires Claude Code on the web.
quote: When you mention `@Claude` with a coding task, Claude automatically detects the intent and creates a Claude Code session on the web... Claude Code in Slack only works in channels (public or private). It does not work in direct messages (DMs).

claim: The community `cc-slack` Go bridge wires three Slack endpoints — `/slack/events` (Events API), `/slack/interactive` (buttons/modals), and `/slack/commands` (a `/cc` slash command) — to spawn the Claude Code CLI per Slack thread, with thread continuity used to resume sessions automatically.
quote: Request URL: https://your-domain/slack/events ... Request URL: https://your-domain/slack/interactive ... Request URL: https://your-domain/slack/commands ... Sessions automatically resume when you return to the same thread

claim: The mpociot Slack bot uses Slack Socket Mode (App-Level Token with `connections:write` plus a Bot User OAuth Token) and drives Claude via the Claude Code SDK directly, streaming responses by editing the Slack message in place as Claude thinks.
quote: Generate a token with `connections:write` scope... bot uses the Claude SDK directly rather than shelling out to a CLI... ⚡ Real-time updates - messages update as Claude thinks 🔄 Streaming responses - see Claude's responses as they're generated

claim: An alternative Cloudflare-Workers-based bot built on Claude Code Action turns each Slack mention into a GitHub issue, lets the GitHub Actions runner execute Claude Code, and uses Cloudflare Queues to poll progress every 10s and post updates back to the Slack thread — the canonical 'GitHub Actions runner + Slack webhook' pattern.
quote: Built with Cloudflare Workers, Hono, and TypeScript on top of Claude Code Action... User mentions bot in Slack → bot creates GitHub issue → Claude Code Action processes the issue asynchronously → Cloudflare Queue monitors progress every 10 seconds → Updates are posted back to Slack thread

claim: The `claude-slack-bridge` MCP server inverts the flow: it exposes an `ask_on_slack` MCP tool that Claude Code calls to pause mid-task, post a question to Slack via a long-lived Socket Mode daemon, and block on a Unix domain socket until the threaded reply returns — letting Slack act as the human-approval channel without polling.
quote: Claude pauses mid-task, asks a question via Slack, waits for your reply, and resumes... Daemon (Docker container, always running): maintains one Slack Socket Mode WebSocket connection and a Unix domain socket server... Zero polling — OS-level blocking I/O

claim: Claude Code exposes six permission modes (default, acceptEdits, plan, auto, dontAsk, bypassPermissions) settable via `--permission-mode`; for headless Slack-driven runs `dontAsk` (only pre-approved tools, fully non-interactive) is the safest CI-style choice and `--dangerously-skip-permissions` is equivalent to `bypassPermissions` and meant for isolated containers only.
quote: `dontAsk` ... Only pre-approved tools ... Locked-down CI and scripts ... `bypassPermissions` ... Everything except protected paths ... Isolated containers and VMs only ... The `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag is equivalent.

claim: In non-interactive (`-p`) mode, `auto` permission mode aborts the session after repeated classifier blocks (3 consecutive or 20 total) since there is no user to prompt — important sizing constraint when an agent is driven from a Slack channel with no TTY.
quote: If the classifier blocks an action 3 times in a row or 20 times total, auto mode pauses... In non-interactive mode with the `-p` flag, repeated blocks abort the session since there is no user to prompt.
